---
title: "TODAY & TeenLABS Proteomics"
author: "Hailey E. Hampson"
date: "2026-03-18"
output: html_document
---
# 1. Set up Libraries & Directores
```{r libraries, echo=F, include = F}
#Load Libraries
library(reticulate)
use_python("/home/hhampson/miniconda3/bin/python", required = TRUE)
source("Lambda_Libraries_Liver_Proteomics.R")

#Load functions
source("Lambda_Liver_Proteomics_Functions.R")
# source("/Users/hhampson/CHCO-Code/Petter Bjornstad/Data Processing and Analysis/Standard_Functions.R")

# install.packages("PGEE")
library(PGEE)
# install.packages("glmnet")
library(glmnet)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)  # For p-values in mixed models
library(dplyr)

```


# 2. Set up Cyberduck to load data
```{r, Cyberduck setup}
## Load boto3 and pandas
boto3 <- reticulate::import("boto3")
pd <- reticulate::import("pandas")

## Create an S3 client
# install.packages("jsonlite")  # Install if not already installed
library(jsonlite)  # Load the package

keys <- fromJSON("/home/hhampson/keys.json") # replace with your Lambda username
session <- boto3$session$Session(
  aws_access_key_id = keys$MY_ACCESS_KEY,
  aws_secret_access_key = keys$MY_SECRET_KEY
)

## Create an S3 client with the session
s3 <- session$client("s3", endpoint_url = "https://s3.kopah.uw.edu")

```

#3. TODAY
## a. Load Data & Format
```{r}
#Load in Proteomics in Today - baseline and follow up & alt ast
gc()
bucket <- "harmonized.dataset" # bucket name in Kopah
temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds") # need to create a temporary file
s3$download_file(bucket, "today_proteomics_longitudinal_log2.rds", temp_file)
TODAY_prot <- readRDS(temp_file)
gc()

proteins <- colnames(TODAY_prot)[which(grepl("seq.",colnames(TODAY_prot)))] #7728 aptamers

#Participants with proteomics
prot_IDs <- unique(TODAY_prot$releaseid)
length(prot_IDs) #379

#Load gene protein key
gc()
bucket <- "harmonized.dataset" # bucket name in Kopah
temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".RDS") # need to create a temporary file
s3$download_file(bucket, "analytes.RDS", temp_file)
analytes <- readRDS(temp_file)
gc()
analytes <- analytes %>% 
  dplyr::select(all_of(c("AptName","EntrezGeneSymbol"))) #7288 aptamers


#Load ALT/AST longitudinal data
#Need to figure out which timeponts we have ALT/AST for and where that data is and then merge it into prot dataframe. 
#Proteins at baseline & 10 years
#ALT/AST at baseline & additional timepoints up to 10 years?

#Research Question: Or impact of change ALT/AST levels on changing proteins over time?Impact of protein changes on ALT/AST Changes over time? 

#Baseline - M00
#Year 10 - P120

#Spaghetti plot of peoples ALT and AST trajectories over time - pick a trajectory type (linear,sline, quadradic) 
#Then get a slope for each pewrsons ALT trajectory
#Slope variable ALT/AST - single value as the outcome
#longitudinal proteins for the PGEE 
#Load in ALT/AST data
gc()
bucket <- "harmonized.dataset" # bucket name in Kopah
temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".RDS") # need to create a temporary file
s3$download_file(bucket, "clinical_data_long.RDS", temp_file)
clinical <- readRDS(temp_file)
gc()

#Select only those with proteomics
clinical <- clinical %>% 
  filter(releaseid %in% prot_IDs)
#Order of visits 
clinical$visit <- as.factor(clinical$visit)
unique(clinical$releaseid)

#ALT
alt <- ggplot(clinical,aes(x=visit,y=alt,color=releaseid))+
  # Individual trajectories (spaghetti lines)
  geom_line(aes(group = releaseid), alpha = 0.2) +
  # Mean line
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = median, geom = "line", 
               color = "grey2", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(x = "Visit",
       y = "ALT (U/L)",
       title = "Longitudinal ALT Trajectories",
       subtitle = "Median ALT Levels (Black Line)")+
  # Vertical lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = "P027", linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = "M00", linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = "P120", linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # Labels for vertical lines
  annotate("text", x = "P027", y = Inf, label = "Start of TODAY2", 
           vjust = 1.5, hjust = -0.1, color = "blue", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = "M00", y = Inf, label = "Baseline\nProteomics", 
           vjust = 1.2, hjust = -0.1, color = "grey30", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = "P120", y = Inf, label = "Follow-Up\nProteomics", 
           vjust = 1.2, hjust = -0.1, color = "grey30", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")

#AST
ast <- ggplot(clinical,aes(x=visit,y=ast,color=releaseid))+
  # Individual trajectories (spaghetti lines)
  geom_line(aes(group = releaseid), alpha = 0.2) +
  # Mean line
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = median, geom = "line", 
               color = "grey2", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(x = "Visit",
       y = "ALT (U/L)",
       title = "Longitudinal AST Trajectories in TODAY & TODAY2",
       subtitle = "Median AST Levels (Black Line)")+
  # Vertical lines
  geom_vline(xintercept = "P027", linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = "M00", linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = "P120", linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # Labels for vertical lines
  annotate("text", x = "P027", y = Inf, label = "Start of TODAY2", 
           vjust = 1.5, hjust = -0.1, color = "blue", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = "M00", y = Inf, label = "Baseline\nProteomics", 
           vjust = 1.2, hjust = -0.1, color = "grey30", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = "P120", y = Inf, label = "Follow-Up\nProteomics", 
           vjust = 1.2, hjust = -0.1, color = "grey30", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
plot <- alt/ast
png("/home/hhampson/Results/ALT_AST_Overtime_TODAY.png",res=300,width=3050,height=2100)
print(plot)
dev.off()

#Visit -> Numeric variable
clinical$visit_num
# clinical$visit_numeric <- as.numeric(clinical$visit)

#ALT
alt <- ggplot(clinical,aes(x=visit_num,y=alt,color=releaseid))+
  # Individual trajectories (spaghetti lines)
  geom_line(aes(group = releaseid), alpha = 0.2) +
  # Mean line
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = median, geom = "line", 
               color = "grey2", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(x = "Visit (Months)",
       y = "ALT (U/L)",
       title = "Longitudinal ALT Trajectories TODAY & TODAY2",
       subtitle = "Median ALT Levels (Black Line)")+
  # Vertical lines
  geom_vline(xintercept =  27, linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 60, linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # Labels for vertical lines
  annotate("text", x = 27, y = Inf, label = "Start of TODAY2", 
           vjust = 1.5, hjust = -0.1, color = "blue", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = Inf, label = "Baseline\nProteomics", 
           vjust = 1.2, hjust = -0.1, color = "grey30", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 60, y = Inf, label = "Follow-Up\nProteomics", 
           vjust = 1.2, hjust = -0.1, color = "grey30", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")

#AST
ast <- ggplot(clinical,aes(x=visit_num,y=ast,color=releaseid))+
  # Individual trajectories (spaghetti lines)
  geom_line(aes(group = releaseid), alpha = 0.2) +
  # Mean line
  stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = median, geom = "line", 
               color = "grey2", linewidth = 1) +
  labs(x = "Visit (Months)",
       y = "ALT (U/L)",
       title = "Longitudinal AST Trajectories in TODAY & TODAY2",
       subtitle = "Median AST Levels (Black Line)")+
  # Vertical lines
  geom_vline(xintercept =  27, linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "blue", linewidth = 1) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.8) +
  geom_vline(xintercept = 60, linetype = "dashed", 
             color = "grey50", linewidth = 0.8) +
  # Labels for vertical lines
  annotate("text", x = 27, y = Inf, label = "Start of TODAY2", 
           vjust = 1.5, hjust = -0.1, color = "blue", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 0, y = Inf, label = "Baseline\nProteomics", 
           vjust = 1.2, hjust = -0.1, color = "grey30", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  annotate("text", x = 60, y = Inf, label = "Follow-Up\nProteomics", 
           vjust = 1.2, hjust = -0.1, color = "grey30", size = 3, angle = 0) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1),
        legend.position = "none")
plot <- alt/ast
png("/home/hhampson/Results/ALT_AST_Overtime_TODAY_Months.png",res=300,width=3050,height=2100)
print(plot)
dev.off()
```
## a. Prepare TODAY Dataset
```{r}
################################################################################
# TODAY PROTEOMICS ANALYSIS
# Author: Hailey E. Hampson
# Date: 2026-03-18
# Description: Clean & process TODAY proteomics + clinical data into analysis
#              datasets, with longitudinal visualization, elastic net feature
#              selection, and mixed effects model validation.
################################################################################

# ── 0. LIBRARIES ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
library(reticulate)
use_python("/home/hhampson/miniconda3/bin/python", required = TRUE)
source("Lambda_Libraries_Liver_Proteomics.R")
source("Lambda_Liver_Proteomics_Functions.R")

library(PGEE)
library(glmnet)
library(lme4)
library(lmerTest)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(jsonlite)

# ── 1. LOAD DATA FROM KOPAH ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

## 1a. S3 / Cyberduck setup ---------------------------------------------------
boto3 <- reticulate::import("boto3")
pd    <- reticulate::import("pandas")

keys    <- fromJSON("/home/hhampson/keys.json")
session <- boto3$session$Session(
  aws_access_key_id     = keys$MY_ACCESS_KEY,
  aws_secret_access_key = keys$MY_SECRET_KEY
)
s3 <- session$client("s3", endpoint_url = "https://s3.kopah.uw.edu")
bucket <- "harmonized.dataset"

## 1b. Proteomics (log2-transformed, long format: one row per person-visit) ---
gc()
temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".rds")
s3$download_file(bucket, "today_proteomics_longitudinal_log2.rds", temp_file)
TODAY_prot <- readRDS(temp_file)
gc()

# Identify aptamer columns
proteins  <- colnames(TODAY_prot)[grepl("^seq\\.", colnames(TODAY_prot))]
cat("Aptamers:", length(proteins), "\n")                        # expect ~7,728

# Participants with proteomics
prot_IDs <- unique(TODAY_prot$releaseid)
cat("Participants with proteomics:", length(prot_IDs), "\n")   # expect ~379

## 1c. Analyte key (AptName → EntrezGeneSymbol) --------------------------------
gc()
temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".RDS")
s3$download_file(bucket, "analytes.RDS", temp_file)
analytes <- readRDS(temp_file) %>%
  dplyr::select(AptName, EntrezGeneSymbol)
gc()
cat("Analytes in key:", nrow(analytes), "\n")                  # expect ~7,288

## 1d. Longitudinal clinical data (ALT, AST, + covariates) --------------------
gc()
temp_file <- tempfile(fileext = ".RDS")
s3$download_file(bucket, "clinical_data_long.RDS", temp_file)
clinical_raw <- readRDS(temp_file)
gc()

# Keep only participants who also have proteomics
clinical_raw <- clinical_raw %>%
  filter(releaseid %in% prot_IDs)
cat("Clinical rows (proteomics participants):", nrow(clinical_raw), "\n")

################################################################################
# 2. DEFINE VISIT ORDERING & NUMERIC TIME
################################################################################

# TODAY visit labels (adjust if your data uses different names)
visit_order <- c(
  "M00",  "M02",  "M06",  "M12",  "M24",  "M36",
  "M48",  "M60",  "M72",  "M84",  "M96",  "M108", "M120",
  "P027", "P039", "P051", "P063", "P075", "P087",
  "P099", "P111", "P120"
)

# Keep only visits that actually appear in the data
visit_order <- visit_order[visit_order %in% unique(clinical_raw$visit)]

# Approximate months from baseline for plotting / mixed models
visit_to_months <- setNames(
  c(0, 2, 6, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60, 72, 84, 96, 108, 120,
    27, 39, 51, 63, 75, 87, 99, 111, 120)[seq_along(visit_order)],
  visit_order
)

################################################################################
# 3. CLEAN CLINICAL DATA
################################################################################

clinical <- clinical_raw %>%
  mutate(
    visit        = factor(visit, levels = visit_order),
    time_months  = visit_to_months[as.character(visit)],
    # Log-transform ALT and AST (natural log; use log2 if preferred)
    log_alt      = log(alt + 1),
    log_ast      = log(ast + 1)
  ) %>%
  # Require at least one non-missing ALT or AST value
  filter(!is.na(alt) | !is.na(ast)) %>%
  arrange(releaseid, visit)

cat("\nClinical data dimensions:", nrow(clinical), "rows x", ncol(clinical), "cols\n")
cat("Unique participants:", length(unique(clinical$releaseid)), "\n")
cat("Visits present:", paste(levels(clinical$visit), collapse = ", "), "\n")

################################################################################
# 4. INDIVIDUAL-LEVEL SUMMARY STATISTICS (for elastic net outcomes)
################################################################################
# Each person → one row with: mean, SD, slope, CV for ALT and AST.
# These become the OUTCOMES in the elastic net feature selection stage.

individual_summaries <- clinical %>%
  group_by(releaseid) %>%
  summarise(
    # ── ALT ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    n_alt       = sum(!is.na(alt)),
    mean_alt    = mean(alt,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_alt      = sd(alt,     na.rm = TRUE),
    cv_alt      = sd_alt / mean_alt,
    # Slope: fit lm(alt ~ time_months) per person; require ≥ 3 visits
    alt_slope   = if (sum(!is.na(alt)) >= 3) {
      coef(lm(alt ~ time_months, data = cur_data(), na.action = na.exclude))[2]
    } else NA_real_,
    # ── AST ──────────────────────────────────────────────
    n_ast       = sum(!is.na(ast)),
    mean_ast    = mean(ast,   na.rm = TRUE),
    sd_ast      = sd(ast,     na.rm = TRUE),
    cv_ast      = sd_ast / mean_ast,
    ast_slope   = if (sum(!is.na(ast)) >= 3) {
      coef(lm(ast ~ time_months, data = cur_data(), na.action = na.exclude))[2]
    } else NA_real_,
    .groups = "drop"
  )

cat("\nIndividual summaries:", nrow(individual_summaries), "participants\n")
cat("Missing alt slopes:", sum(is.na(individual_summaries$alt_slope)), "\n")
cat("Missing ast slopes:", sum(is.na(individual_summaries$ast_slope)), "\n")

################################################################################
# 5. CLEAN PROTEOMICS DATA
################################################################################

# TODAY proteomics has TWO timepoints: baseline (M00) and 10-year (P120).
# Pivot so each participant has: baseline protein values + change scores.

## 5a. Separate baseline and follow-up rows ------------------------------------
prot_baseline <- TODAY_prot %>%
  filter(visit == "M00") %>%           # Adjust label if needed
  dplyr::select(releaseid, all_of(proteins)) %>%
  rename_with(~ ., all_of(proteins))   # no rename needed at baseline

prot_followup <- TODAY_prot %>%
  filter(visit == "P120") %>%          # Adjust label if needed
  dplyr::select(releaseid, all_of(proteins))

cat("\nBaseline prot rows:", nrow(prot_baseline),
    " | Follow-up prot rows:", nrow(prot_followup), "\n")

## 5b. Compute change scores (follow-up minus baseline) -----------------------
# Only for participants with both timepoints
prot_change <- prot_baseline %>%
  inner_join(prot_followup, by = "releaseid", suffix = c("_base", "_fu")) %>%
  {
    base_cols <- paste0(proteins, "_base")
    fu_cols   <- paste0(proteins, "_fu")
    diff_mat  <- as.matrix(dplyr::select(., all_of(fu_cols))) -
                 as.matrix(dplyr::select(., all_of(base_cols)))
    colnames(diff_mat) <- paste0(proteins, "_change")
    bind_cols(dplyr::select(., releaseid), as_tibble(diff_mat))
  }

cat("Participants with both prot timepoints:", nrow(prot_change), "\n")

## 5c. Scale baseline proteins (each protein → mean 0, SD 1) -----------------
# Scaling is done across participants at baseline (cross-sectional).
scale_proteins <- function(df, protein_cols) {
  df[protein_cols] <- scale(df[protein_cols])
  df
}

prot_baseline_scaled <- scale_proteins(prot_baseline, proteins)
prot_change_scaled   <- scale_proteins(prot_change,   paste0(proteins, "_change"))

################################################################################
# 6. BUILD ANALYSIS DATASETS
################################################################################
# Three flavors for elastic net:
#   A. Baseline proteins only
#   B. Change scores only
#   C. Combined (baseline + change)
# All joined to individual-level outcome summaries.

outcomes_cols <- c(
  "mean_alt", "sd_alt", "alt_slope", "cv_alt",
  "mean_ast", "sd_ast", "ast_slope", "cv_ast"
)

## Dataset A: Baseline proteins ------------------------------------------------
analysis_baseline <- prot_baseline_scaled %>%
  inner_join(individual_summaries %>% dplyr::select(releaseid, all_of(outcomes_cols)),
             by = "releaseid")

## Dataset B: Change scores only -----------------------------------------------
change_proteins <- paste0(proteins, "_change")

analysis_change <- prot_change_scaled %>%
  inner_join(individual_summaries %>% dplyr::select(releaseid, all_of(outcomes_cols)),
             by = "releaseid")

## Dataset C: Combined ---------------------------------------------------------
analysis_combined <- prot_baseline_scaled %>%
  inner_join(prot_change_scaled,        by = "releaseid") %>%
  inner_join(individual_summaries %>% dplyr::select(releaseid, all_of(outcomes_cols)),
             by = "releaseid")

cat("\n── Analysis Dataset Dimensions ──\n")
cat("A (baseline):  ", nrow(analysis_baseline),  "×", ncol(analysis_baseline),  "\n")
cat("B (change):    ", nrow(analysis_change),     "×", ncol(analysis_change),     "\n")
cat("C (combined):  ", nrow(analysis_combined),   "×", ncol(analysis_combined),   "\n")

################################################################################
# 7. LONGITUDINAL VISUALIZATION
################################################################################
# Spaghetti plots for ALT and AST with median overlay.
# Data: clinical (long format), with ordered visit factor + numeric time.

## Helper: spaghetti plot function ---------------------------------------------
plot_spaghetti <- function(data, y_var, y_label, title_suffix = "") {
  ggplot(data %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[y_var]])),
         aes(x = time_months, y = .data[[y_var]], group = releaseid)) +
    # Individual trajectories
    geom_line(alpha = 0.15, color = "steelblue", linewidth = 0.4) +
    geom_point(alpha = 0.10, color = "steelblue", size = 0.6) +
    # Median trajectory
    stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = median, geom = "line",
                 color = "#D55E00", linewidth = 1.2) +
    stat_summary(aes(group = 1), fun = median, geom = "point",
                 color = "#D55E00", size = 2.5) +
    # IQR ribbon
    stat_summary(aes(group = 1, ymin = after_stat(y), ymax = after_stat(y)),
                 fun.min = ~ quantile(.x, 0.25, na.rm = TRUE),
                 fun.max = ~ quantile(.x, 0.75, na.rm = TRUE),
                 geom = "ribbon", alpha = 0.15, fill = "#D55E00") +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = sort(unique(data$time_months))) +
    labs(
      x     = "Time (months from baseline)",
      y     = y_label,
      title = paste("Longitudinal", y_label, "Trajectories", title_suffix),
      subtitle = "Thin lines: individual participants | Bold orange: median (IQR ribbon)"
    ) +
    theme_bw(base_size = 12) +
    theme(
      panel.grid.minor = element_blank(),
      axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1)
    )
}

p_alt <- plot_spaghetti(clinical, "alt", "ALT (U/L)", "– TODAY")
p_ast <- plot_spaghetti(clinical, "ast", "AST (U/L)", "– TODAY")

ggsave("TODAY_ALT_spaghetti.png", plot = p_alt, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("TODAY_AST_spaghetti.png", plot = p_ast, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

cat("\nSpaghetti plots saved.\n")

## Log-transformed versions ----------------------------------------------------
p_logalt <- plot_spaghetti(clinical, "log_alt", "log(ALT + 1)", "– TODAY")
p_logast <- plot_spaghetti(clinical, "log_ast", "log(AST + 1)", "– TODAY")
ggsave("TODAY_logALT_spaghetti.png", plot = p_logalt, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)
ggsave("TODAY_logAST_spaghetti.png", plot = p_logast, width = 10, height = 6, dpi = 300)

################################################################################
# 8. ELASTIC NET FEATURE SELECTION
################################################################################
# Run separately for each outcome × analysis dataset combination.
# Returns selected protein names (non-zero coefficients at lambda.min).

run_elastic_net <- function(data, protein_cols, outcome, alpha = 0.5, nfolds = 10,
                            seed = 42) {
  # Remove rows missing the outcome
  df <- data %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome]]))
  if (nrow(df) < 50) {
    warning("Fewer than 50 observations for outcome: ", outcome, ". Skipping.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  X <- as.matrix(df[, protein_cols])
  y <- df[[outcome]]

  # Remove any protein columns with zero variance (can happen after subsetting)
  keep <- apply(X, 2, sd) > 0
  X    <- X[, keep, drop = FALSE]

  set.seed(seed)
  cv_fit <- cv.glmnet(X, y, alpha = alpha, nfolds = nfolds,
                      type.measure = "mse", standardize = FALSE)
  # NOTE: standardize = FALSE because proteins are already scaled

  # Extract non-zero coefficients at lambda.min
  coefs      <- coef(cv_fit, s = "lambda.min")
  coef_df    <- as.data.frame(as.matrix(coefs))
  colnames(coef_df) <- "coefficient"
  coef_df$protein   <- rownames(coef_df)
  selected   <- coef_df %>%
    filter(protein != "(Intercept)", coefficient != 0) %>%
    arrange(desc(abs(coefficient)))

  list(
    cv_fit      = cv_fit,
    lambda_min  = cv_fit$lambda.min,
    cvm_min     = min(cv_fit$cvm),           # Cross-validated MSE
    n_selected  = nrow(selected),
    selected    = selected,
    outcome     = outcome
  )
}

## Define which outcomes to run (focus: mean & slope; can expand) --------------
outcomes_to_run <- c("mean_alt", "alt_slope", "mean_ast", "ast_slope")

cat("\n── Running Elastic Net ──\n")

## Analysis A: Baseline proteins -----------------------------------------------
en_results_A <- lapply(outcomes_to_run, function(oc) {
  cat("  Baseline |", oc, "\n")
  run_elastic_net(analysis_baseline, proteins, oc)
})
names(en_results_A) <- outcomes_to_run

## Analysis C: Combined proteins -----------------------------------------------
all_proteins_C <- c(proteins, change_proteins)

en_results_C <- lapply(outcomes_to_run, function(oc) {
  cat("  Combined |", oc, "\n")
  run_elastic_net(analysis_combined, all_proteins_C, oc)
})
names(en_results_C) <- outcomes_to_run

## Summary table ---------------------------------------------------------------
make_en_summary <- function(results_list, analysis_label) {
  do.call(rbind, lapply(names(results_list), function(oc) {
    res <- results_list[[oc]]
    if (is.null(res)) return(NULL)
    data.frame(
      analysis    = analysis_label,
      outcome     = oc,
      n_obs       = nrow(analysis_baseline %>% filter(!is.na(.data[[oc]]))),
      lambda_min  = round(res$lambda_min,  4),
      cv_mse      = round(res$cvm_min,     3),
      n_selected  = res$n_selected
    )
  }))
}

en_summary <- bind_rows(
  make_en_summary(en_results_A, "Baseline"),
  make_en_summary(en_results_C, "Combined")
)

print(en_summary)
write.csv(en_summary, "TODAY_elastic_net_summary.csv", row.names = FALSE)

## Annotate selected proteins with gene names ----------------------------------
annotate_selected <- function(selected_df, analytes_df) {
  selected_df %>%
    left_join(analytes_df, by = c("protein" = "AptName"))
}

# Example: annotate mean_alt baseline results
if (!is.null(en_results_A[["mean_alt"]])) {
  mean_alt_selected_A <- annotate_selected(
    en_results_A[["mean_alt"]]$selected, analytes
  )
  cat("\nTop 20 proteins for mean ALT (Baseline approach):\n")
  print(head(mean_alt_selected_A, 20))
  write.csv(mean_alt_selected_A, "TODAY_mean_alt_baseline_selected_proteins.csv",
            row.names = FALSE)
}

################################################################################
# 9. MIXED EFFECTS MODEL VALIDATION
################################################################################
# For each outcome's selected proteins, run a proper longitudinal mixed model:
#   outcome_raw_longitudinal ~ time + protein_1 + ... + protein_k + (1 + time | id)
#
# The clinical data is in long format (multiple rows per person).
# Selected proteins are merged in from the BASELINE (wide) proteomics data.

run_mixed_models <- function(selected_proteins_df,     # data.frame: protein, coefficient, EntrezGeneSymbol
                             clinical_long,             # long-format clinical data
                             prot_baseline_wide,        # wide: one row per person, baseline proteins
                             outcome_col,               # "alt" or "ast"
                             max_proteins = 30,         # safety cap for convergence
                             random_slope  = TRUE) {

  sel_prots <- head(selected_proteins_df$protein, max_proteins)
  if (length(sel_prots) == 0) {
    message("No proteins selected for outcome: ", outcome_col)
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Merge baseline proteins into long clinical data
  model_data <- clinical_long %>%
    filter(!is.na(.data[[outcome_col]])) %>%
    left_join(prot_baseline_wide %>% dplyr::select(releaseid, all_of(sel_prots)),
              by = "releaseid") %>%
    filter(complete.cases(dplyr::select(., all_of(sel_prots))))

  if (nrow(model_data) < 100) {
    warning("Too few complete observations for mixed model. Skipping.")
    return(NULL)
  }

  # Build formula
  fixed_terms <- paste(c("time_months", sel_prots), collapse = " + ")
  re_term     <- if (random_slope) "(1 + time_months | releaseid)" else "(1 | releaseid)"
  formula_str <- paste0(outcome_col, " ~ ", fixed_terms, " + ", re_term)

  cat("  Fitting:", formula_str, "\n")

  fit <- tryCatch(
    lmer(as.formula(formula_str), data = model_data, REML = FALSE,
         control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                               optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))),
    error = function(e) {
      message("  Model failed: ", e$message)
      # Fall back: random intercept only
      formula_ri <- paste0(outcome_col, " ~ ", fixed_terms, " + (1 | releaseid)")
      tryCatch(
        lmer(as.formula(formula_ri), data = model_data, REML = FALSE,
             control = lmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa",
                                   optCtrl   = list(maxfun = 2e5))),
        error = function(e2) { message("  Fallback also failed: ", e2$message); NULL }
      )
    }
  )

  if (is.null(fit)) return(NULL)

  # Extract fixed effects table (lmerTest provides p-values)
  coef_table <- as.data.frame(summary(fit)$coefficients)
  coef_table$term    <- rownames(coef_table)
  coef_table$outcome <- outcome_col

  # FDR correction across protein terms only
  prot_rows   <- coef_table$term %in% sel_prots
  coef_table$fdr <- NA_real_
  if (any(prot_rows)) {
    coef_table$fdr[prot_rows] <- p.adjust(coef_table[prot_rows, "Pr(>|t|)"],
                                           method = "BH")
  }

  # Annotate gene symbols
  coef_table <- coef_table %>%
    left_join(analytes, by = c("term" = "AptName"))

  list(model = fit, results = coef_table)
}

## Run mixed models for mean ALT (Baseline approach) --------------------------
cat("\n── Mixed Effects Models: Mean ALT (Baseline proteins) ──\n")

if (!is.null(en_results_A[["mean_alt"]])) {
  mm_mean_alt_A <- run_mixed_models(
    selected_proteins_df = en_results_A[["mean_alt"]]$selected,
    clinical_long        = clinical,
    prot_baseline_wide   = prot_baseline_scaled,   # scaled; interpretable as SD units
    outcome_col          = "alt",
    max_proteins         = 30,
    random_slope         = TRUE
  )

  if (!is.null(mm_mean_alt_A)) {
    sig_proteins <- mm_mean_alt_A$results %>%
      filter(!is.na(fdr), fdr < 0.05) %>%
      arrange(fdr)
    cat("Significant proteins (FDR < 0.05) for ALT:\n")
    print(sig_proteins %>% dplyr::select(term, EntrezGeneSymbol, Estimate,
                                          `Std. Error`, `t value`, `Pr(>|t|)`, fdr))
    write.csv(mm_mean_alt_A$results,
              "TODAY_mixed_model_mean_alt_baseline.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  }
}

## Run mixed models for mean AST (Baseline approach) --------------------------
cat("\n── Mixed Effects Models: Mean AST (Baseline proteins) ──\n")

if (!is.null(en_results_A[["mean_ast"]])) {
  mm_mean_ast_A <- run_mixed_models(
    selected_proteins_df = en_results_A[["mean_ast"]]$selected,
    clinical_long        = clinical,
    prot_baseline_wide   = prot_baseline_scaled,
    outcome_col          = "ast",
    max_proteins         = 30,
    random_slope         = TRUE
  )

  if (!is.null(mm_mean_ast_A)) {
    sig_proteins <- mm_mean_ast_A$results %>%
      filter(!is.na(fdr), fdr < 0.05) %>%
      arrange(fdr)
    cat("Significant proteins (FDR < 0.05) for AST:\n")
    print(sig_proteins %>% dplyr::select(term, EntrezGeneSymbol, Estimate,
                                          `Std. Error`, `t value`, `Pr(>|t|)`, fdr))
    write.csv(mm_mean_ast_A$results,
              "TODAY_mixed_model_mean_ast_baseline.csv", row.names = FALSE)
  }
}

################################################################################
# 10. SAVE CLEAN ANALYSIS DATASETS
################################################################################

saveRDS(clinical,             "TODAY_clinical_long_clean.rds")
saveRDS(individual_summaries, "TODAY_individual_summaries.rds")
saveRDS(prot_baseline_scaled, "TODAY_prot_baseline_scaled.rds")
saveRDS(prot_change_scaled,   "TODAY_prot_change_scaled.rds")
saveRDS(analysis_baseline,    "TODAY_analysis_baseline.rds")
saveRDS(analysis_change,      "TODAY_analysis_change.rds")
saveRDS(analysis_combined,    "TODAY_analysis_combined.rds")

cat("\n────────────────────────────────────────────\n")
cat("TODAY datasets saved:\n")
cat("  TODAY_clinical_long_clean.rds       — long-format clinical (all visits)\n")
cat("  TODAY_individual_summaries.rds      — one row per person (mean/SD/slope outcomes)\n")
cat("  TODAY_prot_baseline_scaled.rds      — scaled baseline proteins (wide)\n")
cat("  TODAY_prot_change_scaled.rds        — scaled change proteins (wide)\n")
cat("  TODAY_analysis_baseline.rds         — elastic net input: baseline proteins + outcomes\n")
cat("  TODAY_analysis_change.rds           — elastic net input: change proteins + outcomes\n")
cat("  TODAY_analysis_combined.rds         — elastic net input: baseline + change + outcomes\n")
cat("────────────────────────────────────────────\n")


```
